opp.name: IAG_SRM
opp.next_step: Hi Casper, Good to hear from you. We would like to involve the OpCos at this stage by asking them to review the shortlisted suppliers and provide input into the final selection. We are currently confirming a few time options with BA and will share confirmed dates shortly. In the meantime, it would be helpful for you to be prepared to present to BA in the near term, with a primary focus on demonstrating functionality.

sources: Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point note: 'Businesses must manually input all data for a new vendor if it is not included in Merlin' (speaker_id 1)

Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point note: 'A supplier is unable to find the status of an invoice and needs a way to raise a ticket to contact an internal team for assistance' (speaker_id 6, Linda Bode context)

Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point note: 'Swissport may fail to update their bank account information in the system, leading to discrepancies when invoices are received with new bank details' (speaker_id 1)
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated several operational pain points on the 25 Jun 2026 demo call, but no quantified financial impact has been stated. Kelly Eastman-Thompson's team (Category Lead - IT Software) confirmed that businesses must manually input all data for new vendors not included in Merlin, which creates inefficiency (quote: 'business have to manually introduce all the data for the new vendor if it is not included in the in Merlin?', 25 Jun). Linda Bode (British Airways) raised a pain point that suppliers cannot find invoice status and need to raise tickets to contact internal teams for assistance, indicating a supplier-experience gap. A further pain was noted around Swissport failing to update bank account information in the system, leading to discrepancies when invoices arrive with new bank details. No cycle-time savings, headcount reduction, or ROI figures have been quantified. The immediate move is to ask the Economic Buyer (once identified) to quantify the operational cost of these inefficiencies and the expected ROI from automating supplier onboarding and bank validation.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Kelly Eastman-Thompson, Category Lead - IT Software, kelly.eastmanthompson@iaggbs.com
SF Next_Step__c: message addressed to Casper, indicating Kelly is the active buyer contact coordinating OpCo reviews and BA demo
Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026: Kelly Eastman-Thompson attended (kelly.eastmanthompson@iaggbs.com in attendee list)
SF LastActivityDate: 2026-06-25 (Kelly engagement is current)
champion.narrative: Kelly Eastman-Thompson (Category Lead - IT Software) is the primary contact and appears to be the process owner for this evaluation. She attended the 25 Jun 2026 demo call and is named in the Next Step message as the recipient of Casper's outreach ('Hi Casper, Good to hear from you'). She is actively coordinating OpCo involvement and scheduling the BA demo. Evidence of advocacy: she is driving the multi-OpCo review process and has confirmed near-term next steps. What is missing: no evidence yet that she has introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, no evidence of internal selling on our behalf, and no confirmation that she has access to or influence over the final decision-maker. She is engaged and coordinating the evaluation, but her role appears to be evaluation-project-lead rather than executive sponsor or champion with budget authority. Champion strength is developing because she is active and coordinating, but we have not yet seen her push back on competitors, share internal docs, or escalate us to the EB. The move is to ask her directly who the Economic Buyer is, request an introduction, and ask her to share how she sees Zycus fitting IAG's strategy so we can co-develop the internal business case.

sources: SF Competitors__c: null (ground truth)
SF Next_Step__c: 'review the shortlisted suppliers' (plural, confirms multi-vendor evaluation but does not name competitors)
Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026: Casper question 'How's it been watching all of these demos and looking at different providers?' (speaker_id 2, no buyer answer captured in notes)
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is null (per ground truth 'unknown'). The 25 Jun 2026 demo call and Next Step message make no reference to other vendors being evaluated. The Next Step message states that IAG GBS will ask OpCos to 'review the shortlisted suppliers' (plural), which confirms Zycus is one of multiple vendors under consideration, but does not name the others. On the demo call, Casper asked 'How's it been watching all of these demos and looking at different providers?' (speaker_id 2), and later 'Are you working with someone already?' (speaker_id 2, in the context of bank validation bureaus, not competitive SRM platforms), but no buyer response naming a competitor was captured in the notes. The unknowns are: (1) who else is shortlisted, (2) whether an incumbent supplier-portal or SRM system exists (the manual-data-entry pain suggests no robust platform today), and (3) whether this is a competitive RFP or a sole-source evaluation with fallback options. The immediate move is to ask Kelly Eastman-Thompson directly who else is being evaluated and what the current state (incumbent, manual process, or another platform) looks like, so we can position our wedge.

sources: Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point: 'Businesses must manually input all data for a new vendor if it is not included in Merlin' (speaker_id 1)

Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point: 'There is a lack of a mechanism to prevent suppliers from submitting insufficient information, which leads to inefficiencies in the onboarding process' (speaker_id 6)
Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point: 'A supplier is unable to find the status of an invoice and needs a way to raise a ticket to contact an internal team for assistance' (speaker_id 6)

Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026, pain_point: 'Swissport may fail to update their bank account information in the system, leading to discrepancies when invoices are received with new bank details' (speaker_id 1)
identify_pain.narrative: The buyer confirmed multiple operational pain points on the 25 Jun 2026 demo call. The most concrete pain is manual data entry for new vendors: 'business have to manually introduce all the data for the new vendor if it is not included in the in Merlin?' (speaker_id 1, confirmed by Nishita Sareen context). A second pain is the lack of alternative bank account validation when TrustPay coverage is insufficient: 'If the system is not able to check a bank account, do you have any alternative way to do this check?' (speaker_id 1). A third pain is inefficiency in supplier onboarding due to incomplete submissions: 'There is a lack of a mechanism to prevent suppliers from submitting insufficient information, which leads to inefficiencies in the onboarding process' (speaker_id 6, Linda Bode context). A fourth pain is supplier invoice-status visibility: 'A supplier is unable to find the status of an invoice and needs a way to raise a ticket to contact an internal team for assistance' (speaker_id 6). A fifth pain is bank account update lag: 'Swissport may fail to update their bank account information in the system, leading to discrepancies when invoices are received with new bank details' (speaker_id 1). The pain owner is Kelly Eastman-Thompson (Category Lead - IT Software) and her cross-OpCo team, supported by operational contacts at BA, Iberia, and Vueling. The confirmed pain is operational inefficiency in supplier lifecycle management (onboarding, data quality, bank validation, invoice collaboration); the inferred pain (not yet quantified) is the cost of manual workarounds and supplier friction.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel) have been discussed or documented. The 25 Jun demo call focused entirely on product functionality and supplier-workflow questions; no legal, security, or procurement topics were raised. No SI or partner involvement has been mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is populated in Salesforce, so there is no known forcing function. At Formal Evaluation stage, 6 months into the cycle and 188 days from close, the absence of any paper-process visibility is a moderate risk. The move is to ask Kelly Eastman-Thompson what the contracting process looks like (legal, InfoSec, procurement approvals), whether an MSA is required, and what the typical cycle time is from vendor selection to contract signature at IAG.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole query: 2 buyer-side roles, neither marked Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor
Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026 attendee list: no CFO, Head of Procurement, or C-level sponsor present
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. The 25 Jun 2026 demo call included Category Lead Kelly Eastman-Thompson and operational contacts from British Airways (Linda Bode, Jennifer Willis), Iberia (mjalmendros, crodrigueza), Vueling (Irene Dominguez, Anna Buruaga), and Piotr Banasik (Loyalty & Customer Operations Manager), but no CFO, Head of Procurement, or C-level sponsor participated. The Next Step message references involving OpCos in the supplier review, but does not name who will approve budget or sign the contract. The Salesforce opportunity carries no Contact Role marked Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. At this stage (Formal Evaluation, 6 months in, 188 days to close) the absence of EB engagement is a critical gap. The immediate move is to ask Kelly Eastman-Thompson who will approve the budget and sign the contract, and to request an introduction to that person within the next 14 days.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: 'involve the OpCos at this stage by asking them to review the shortlisted suppliers and provide input into the final selection; We are currently confirming a few time options with BA'
Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026: Casper question 'when we hopefully qualify to next stage where there potentially is a proof of concept' (speaker_id 2)

Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026 attendee list: British Airways (Linda Bode, Jennifer Willis), Iberia (mjalmendros, crodrigueza), Vueling (Irene Dominguez, Anna Buruaga) all present, indicating federated evaluation
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is multi-OpCo and staged. The Next Step message (latest update, post-25 Jun demo) states that IAG GBS will 'involve the OpCos at this stage by asking them to review the shortlisted suppliers and provide input into the final selection', indicating a federated evaluation where each operating company (British Airways, Iberia, Vueling) has input. British Airways is scheduling a near-term functionality-focused demo (dates being confirmed). The 25 Jun demo call included representatives from all three OpCos, suggesting parallel evaluation tracks. Casper Hoeholt (Zycus owner) asked on the call 'when we hopefully qualify to next stage where there potentially is a proof of concept', signaling that a POC stage may follow shortlisting. No formal approval chain, contract authority, or procurement-committee structure has been documented. The unknowns are: (1) who aggregates OpCo feedback and makes the final decision, (2) whether each OpCo signs separately or IAG GBS consolidates, (3) the timeline from BA demo to shortlist decision, and (4) whether a POC is mandatory or optional. The move is to ask Kelly Eastman-Thompson to map the decision process (OpCo input aggregation, final decision-maker, POC gate) and the approval chain.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: 'primary focus on demonstrating functionality' (BA OpCo demo request)

Avoma demo call 25 Jun 2026: questions on bank validation ('Do you have the ability to validate any bank account in any country of the world?', speaker_id 3), sanctions ('sanctions from a peer-to-peer perspective to avoid cash being trapped in sanctioned companies', speaker_id 6 objection), approval flow ('what's the approval flow for that particular process?', speaker_id 3), supplier portal cost ('From a supplier perspective, this portal has any cost?', speaker_id 3), and invoice status visibility ('A supplier is unable to find the status of an invoice', pain_point speaker_id 6)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are partially visible from the 25 Jun 2026 demo and the Next Step message. The BA OpCo has requested a demo with a 'primary focus on demonstrating functionality', signaling that functional fit is a core criterion. On the 25 Jun call, buyers asked detailed questions about supplier onboarding workflow, bank account validation coverage (including BIC/tax ID validation), approval-flow configurability, risk assessment integration, TrustPay bureau integration and alternatives, sanctions screening (from a peer-to-peer perspective to avoid cash trapped in sanctioned companies, per Linda Bode), invoice-status visibility for suppliers, and supplier portal cost. These questions map to a decision framework centered on: (1) functional depth of supplier lifecycle management (onboarding, portal, invoice collaboration), (2) bank validation and sanctions compliance, (3) configurability of approval workflows, and (4) cost to suppliers. No formal RFP document, BRD, or weighted scorecard has been shared. The move is to request the written evaluation criteria or BRD and to confirm whether sanctions screening and bank validation coverage are hard requirements or nice-to-haves.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. Deal is 6 months into Formal Evaluation, 188 days to close, with no CFO, Head of Procurement, or C-level sponsor visibility. Risk: shortlist decision may exclude Zycus if EB is not bought in.
Competition blind. Buyer confirmed multiple 'shortlisted suppliers' under evaluation, but no competitor named. Cannot tailor positioning or wedge without knowing the field.

Single-thread risk. Only 2 buyer-side contact roles (Kelly Eastman-Thompson, Piotr Banasik) despite 7-person multi-OpCo demo on 25 Jun. Five engaged OpCo contacts (BA, Iberia, Vueling) are not OpportunityContactRoles, limiting multi-thread advocacy.

Bank account validation coverage questioned. Buyer asked whether TrustPay can validate any bank account globally and what fallback exists if coverage is insufficient. Risk: if TrustPay coverage is inadequate for IAG's global supplier base, this may be a deal-breaker or require custom integration with another bureau.

Sanctions screening from P2P perspective raised by Linda Bode (BA) to avoid cash trapped in sanctioned companies. No evidence that Zycus sanctions capability was confirmed or aligned to this requirement on the 25 Jun demo.

British Airways OpCo demo dates not yet confirmed. Buyer stated 'We are currently confirming a few time options with BA and will share confirmed dates shortly.' Risk: if BA demo slips beyond July, the evaluation timeline may push the close date.

Supplier portal demo delivered yesterday to multi-OpCo audience (British Airways, Iberia, Vueling representatives). Next step: BA OpCo demo on functionality, dates being confirmed. Pain identified on 25 Jun call: manual data entry for new vendors not in Merlin, lack of bank account validation alternatives, inefficiencies in supplier onboarding (insufficient information submission), and invoice-status visibility gaps for suppliers
north_star_verdict.headline: Multi-OpCo demo delivered yesterday with 7 stakeholders confirms active buyer engagement, but 6 months in Formal Evaluation with only 2 contacts mapped, no EB/DM identified, and unknown shortlisted competitors creates execution risk. Pipeline forecast premature without decision-process clarity.

best_practice_check.summary: Three high-leverage win-strategy actions for this multi-OpCo federated evaluation: (1) Map the Economic Buyer and decision authority before the BA demo (no EB visibility is the single biggest gap for a deal 188 days from close in Formal Evaluation), (2) discover the competitive shortlist so we can tailor the BA functionality demo to our wedge, and (3) multi-thread beyond Kelly Eastman-Thompson to the BA, Iberia, and Vueling operational contacts who attended the 25 Jun demo, so we have OpCo-level champions advocating in parallel during the review phase.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified. The Next Step message confirms Zycus is one of multiple 'shortlisted suppliers' under IAG GBS evaluation, but no rival vendor has been named in any Salesforce field, call, or task. The buyer's pain points (manual vendor data entry, lack of bank validation alternatives, supplier onboarding inefficiencies) suggest no robust incumbent SRM platform is in place today, implying either a greenfield opportunity or displacement of manual/legacy processes. Without knowing the shortlist, the competitive wedge is unclear. The immediate action is to ask Kelly Eastman-Thompson who else is being evaluated and what the current state looks like, so we can position our functional depth (iSource, iSupplier, iRequest integration) and Merlin AI data enrichment as the differentiator.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Partial alignment visible; several buyer requirements raised on 25 Jun demo are addressable by Zycus platform, but gaps exist. Supplier portal cost, sanctions screening from P2P perspective, and invoice-status visibility for suppliers were raised as evaluation criteria. No formal BRD or weighted scorecard shared yet.

deltas.subject: Bank account validation coverage questioned. Buyer asked whether TrustPay can validate any bank account globally and what fallback exists if coverage is insufficient. deltas.to: Single-thread risk: only 2 buyer-side contact roles on this opp (Kelly Eastman-Thompson, Piotr Banasik). The 25 Jun demo had 7 IAG attendees across 3 OpCos (BA:
deltas.subject: Single-thread risk: only 2 buyer-side contact roles on this opp (Kelly Eastman-Thompson, Piotr Banasik). The 25 Jun demo had 7 IAG attendees across 3 OpCos (BA: Linda Bode, Jennifer Willis; Iberia: mjalmendros, crodrigueza; Vueling: Irene Dominguez, Anna Buruaga), but none are OpportunityContactRoles. Move: add these 5 OpCo contacts as roles and multi-thread directly to build OpCo-level advocacy during the review phase.

scope

gaps: No Economic Buyer identified in contact roles or call attendees (knowledge gap)
No competitor named in any source despite buyer confirming multiple shortlisted suppliers (knowledge gap)
No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, procurement approvals) discussed or documented (knowledge gap)
No quantified business case or ROI target despite operational pain confirmed (knowledge gap)
Decision process partially visible (multi-OpCo review confirmed) but final decision-maker and approval chain unknown (knowledge gap)
